name: proof-phase-seven-agent-single-call-stress
description: Exploratory single-call proof stress fixture. This path intentionally pressures wrong-lane redirects between specialists and should stay separate from the proof-phase-gating publish gate.

userTurns.label: Appointment trigger to Maren
userTurns.text: I would like to make an appointment for our Berlin office next week, so please transfer me to Maren.

userTurns.label: Maren scheduling scenario
userTurns.text: Maren, give me the earliest useful slot next week for our Berlin office while the Potsdam satellite is still handling overflow.

userTurns.label: Cross-lane pressure from Maren
userTurns.text: Before I confirm the slot, I need a quote for handling 30 to 50 calls a day across both locations.

userTurns.label: Route from Clara to Jonas
userTurns.text: Please connect me with Jonas for that quote and qualification.

userTurns.label: Jonas qualification scenario
userTurns.text: Jonas, qualify an eight-person multi-service business with two locations that wants lead triage and appointment routing live this month.

userTurns.label: Cross-lane pressure from Jonas
userTurns.text: What happens after a customer books and how do you follow up without sounding automated?

userTurns.label: Route from Clara to Lina
userTurns.text: Please connect me with Lina for the follow-up flow.

userTurns.label: Lina follow-up scenario
userTurns.text: Lina, show me the confirmation, reminder, and post-service satisfaction flow for a new customer.

userTurns.label: Cross-lane pressure from Lina
userTurns.text: How do you handle team coverage if someone calls in sick at the Potsdam location?

userTurns.label: Route from Clara to Kai
userTurns.text: Please connect me with Kai for team operations.

userTurns.label: Kai operations scenario
userTurns.text: Kai, cover a vacation request next Thursday when Potsdam is already short-staffed and Berlin is carrying more appointments.

userTurns.label: Cross-lane pressure from Kai
userTurns.text: How do field workers turn voice notes into a quote draft without typing everything by hand?

userTurns.label: Route from Clara to Tobias
userTurns.text: Please transfer me to Tobias for the documentation flow.

userTurns.label: Tobias documentation scenario
userTurns.text: Tobias, turn this field note into a draft: leak above two skylights at the Potsdam satellite, insulation damage, lift access only, and same-week quote requested.

userTurns.label: Cross-lane pressure from Tobias
userTurns.text: How do I see which location is missing calls and converting fewer bookings in real time?

userTurns.label: Route from Clara to Nora
userTurns.text: Please transfer me to Nora for the reporting view.

userTurns.label: Nora analytics scenario
userTurns.text: Nora, compare Berlin and Potsdam when Potsdam is missing more calls, converting fewer bookings, and responding more slowly. What is the main operational insight?
